summary: Joel announces the first two chapters of his book 'User Interface Design for Programmers.' He observes that most hardcore C++ programmers dislike UI programming, which he finds surprising since he considers it easy, straightforward, and fun. The post introduces a fundamental axiom of good UI design and promises additional chapters in coming weeks.
key_points: Most hardcore C++ programmers dislike UI programming, despite Joel finding it enjoyable and straightforward
Joel introduces a 'fundamental axiom' of good user interface design in Chapter One
Chapter Two applies this axiom with real-life examples, making the concepts practical
money_quotes: Most of the hard core C++ programmers I know hate user interface programming.
This surprises me, because I find UI programming to be quintessentially easy, straightforward, and fun.
key_insight: UI programming doesn't have to be the dreaded task most systems programmers make it out to be — it can be approachable and enjoyable when grounded in clear axioms.
